records.chapter: Submission of Intellect and its Excellence
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad Āṣif al-Muḥsinī
records.englishText: 1. [1/1] Usul al-Kafi: From a number of our companions among them Muhammad b. Yahya al-Attar from Ahmad b. Muhammad from al-Hasan b. Mahbub from al-Ala b. Razin from Muhammad b. Muslim from Abi Ja’far عليه السلام who said: When Allāh created the intellect He gave it the power to speak, then He said to it: draw near, so it drew near, then He said to it: turn back, so it turned back, then He said: by my Power and Majesty I have not created a creature more beloved to me than you, and I have not perfected you except in the one I love, as for me you do I command, and you do I prohibit, and you do I punish and you do I reward.
records.arabicText: [1/1] اصول الكافي: عن عدة من أصحابنا منهم محمد بن يحيى العطار، عن أحمد بن محمد، عن الحسن بن محبوب، عن العلاء بن رزين، عن محمد بن مسلم، عن أبي جعفر عليه السلام قال: لما خلق الله العقل استنطقه ثم قال له: أقبل فأقبل ثم قال له: أدبر فأدبر ثم قال: وعزتي وجلالي ما خلقت خلقا هو أحب إلي منك ولا أكملتك إلا فيمن احب، أما إني إياك آمر، وإياك أنهى وإياك اعاقب، وإياك اثيب
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. [1/1] Usul al-Kafi: From a number of our companions among them Muhammad b. Yahya al-Attar from Ahmad b. Muhammad from al-Hasan b. Mahbub from al-Ala b. Razin from Muhammad b. Muslim from Abi Ja’far عليه السلام who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: When Allāh created the intellect He gave it the power to speak, then He said to it: draw near, so it drew near, then He said to it: turn back, so it turned back, then He said: by my Power and Majesty I have not created a creature more beloved to me than you, and I have not perfected you except in the one I love, as for me you do I command, and you do I prohibit, and you do I punish and you do I reward.

records.chapter: Submission of Intellect and its Excellence
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad Āṣif al-Muḥsinī
records.englishText: 2. [2/2] Usul al-Kafi: Muhammad b. Yahya from Ahmad b. Muhammad b. Isa from Ibn Fadhal from al-Hasan b. Jahm who said: I heard al-Ridha عليه السلام saying: the friend of every man is his intellect, and his enemy is his ignorance.
records.arabicText: [2/2] اصول الكافي: محمد بن يحيى، عن أحمد بن محمد بن عيسى، عن ابن فضال، عن الحسن بن الجهم قال: سمعت الرضا عليه السلام يقول: صديق كل امرء عقله، وعدوه جهله
records.thaqalaynSanad: 2. [2/2] Usul al-Kafi: Muhammad b. Yahya from Ahmad b. Muhammad b. Isa from Ibn Fadhal from al-Hasan b. Jahm who said: I heard al-Ridha عليه السلام saying:
records.thaqalaynMatn: the friend of every man is his intellect, and his enemy is his ignorance.

records.chapter: Submission of Intellect and its Excellence
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad Āṣif al-Muḥsinī
records.englishText: 3. [3/3] Usul al-Kafi: From him (Muhammad b. Yahya) from Ahmad b. Muhammad from Ibn Fadhal from al-Hasan b. Jahm who said: I said to Abi al-Hasan عليه السلام: we have a group who have love (towards you) but they are not firm in resolve though they profess this belief (Tashayyu), so he عليه السلام said: these are not those who were admonished by Allāh, when Allāh said: <>(59:2).
records.arabicText: [3/3] اصول الكافي: وعنه، عن أحمد بن محمد، عن ابن فضال، عن الحسن بن الجهم قال: قلت لابي الحسن عليه السلام: إن عندنا قوما لهم محبة، وليست لهم تلك العزيمة يقولون بهذا القول؟ فقال: ليس اولئك ممن عاتب الله إنما قال الله: فاعتبروا يا أولي الابصار
records.thaqalaynSanad: 3. [3/3] Usul al-Kafi: From him (Muhammad b. Yahya) from Ahmad b. Muhammad from Ibn Fadhal from al-Hasan b. Jahm who said: I said to Abi al-Hasan عليه السلام:
records.thaqalaynMatn: we have a group who have love (towards you) but they are not firm in resolve though they profess this belief (Tashayyu), so he عليه السلام said: these are not those who were admonished by Allāh, when Allāh said: <>(59:2).

records.chapter: Submission of Intellect and its Excellence
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad Āṣif al-Muḥsinī
records.englishText: 4. [4/4] Rijal al-Kashshi: Hamduwayh b. Nusayr who said: narrated to us Ayyub b. Nuh from Safwan b. Yahya from Dawud b. Farqad who said: I heard Aba Abdillah عليه السلام saying: my companions are the people of discernment and god-consciousness, whoever is not from the people of discernment and god-consciousness then he is not from my companions.
records.arabicText: [4/4] رجال الكشي: عن حمدويه بن نصيرعن أيوب بن نوح، عن صفوان بن يحيى، عن داود بن فرقد، قال : سمعت أبا عبد الله (عليه السلام) يقول إن أصحابي أولو النهى و التقى فمن لم يكن من أهل النهى و التقى فليس من أصحابي
records.thaqalaynSanad: 4. [4/4] Rijal al-Kashshi: Hamduwayh b. Nusayr who said: narrated to us Ayyub b. Nuh from Safwan b. Yahya from Dawud b. Farqad who said: I heard Aba Abdillah عليه السلام saying:
records.thaqalaynMatn: my companions are the people of discernment and god-consciousness, whoever is not from the people of discernment and god-consciousness then he is not from my companions.

records.chapter: Submission of Intellect and its Excellence
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad Āṣif al-Muḥsinī
records.englishText: 5. [5/5] Usul al-Kafi: Ali from his father (Ibrahim b. Hashim) from Abi Hashim al-Ja’fari who said: We were with al-Ridha عليه السلام when we began discussing about the intellect and moral virtue, so he said: O Abu Hashim, intellect is a free gift from Allāh while virtue is an effortful acquisition, whoever strives to acquire virtue is able to possess it but whoever strives to acquire intellect he achieves naught by that except further ignorance.
records.arabicText: [5/5] اصول الكافي: علي، عن أبيه، عن أبي هاشم الجعفري قال: كنا عند الرضا عليه السلام فتذاكرنا العقل والادب فقال: يا أبا هاشم العقل حباء من الله والادب كلفة، فمن تكلف الادب قدر عليه، ومن تكلف العقل لم يزدد بذلك إلا جهلا
records.thaqalaynSanad: 5. [5/5] Usul al-Kafi: Ali from his father (Ibrahim b. Hashim) from Abi Hashim al-Ja’fari who said: We were with al-Ridha عليه السلام when we began discussing about the intellect and moral virtue, so he said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: O Abu Hashim, intellect is a free gift from Allāh while virtue is an effortful acquisition, whoever strives to acquire virtue is able to possess it but whoever strives to acquire intellect he achieves naught by that except further ignorance.

records.chapter: Submission of Intellect and its Excellence
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad Āṣif al-Muḥsinī
records.englishText: 6. [6/6] Usul al-Kafi: Abu Abdillah al-Asimi from Ali b. al-Hasan from Ali b. Asbat from al-Hasan b. al-Jahm from Abi al-Hasan al-Ridha عليه السلام. He (al-Asimi) said: Our companions (the Shias) and intellect were mentioned in his presence, so he عليه السلام said: consideration is not given to a man of religion who has no intellect. I said: may I be made your ransom, from those who claim this affair (Wilaya) there are some who do mean well but do not have that level of intellect, so he said: such people are not the ones addressed by Allāh, verily Allāh created the intellect and said to it: draw near, so it drew near, and said to it: turn back, so it turned back, then He said: by my power and majesty I have not created a thing better than you or more beloved to me than you, by you do I take and by you do I give.
records.arabicText: [6/6] اصول الكافي: أبوعبدالله العاصمي، عن علي بن الحسن، عن علي بن أسباط، عن الحسن ابن الجهم، عن أبي الحسن الرضا عليه السلام قال: ذكر عنده أصحابنا وذكر العقل قال: فقال عليه السلام: لا يعبأ بأهل الدين ممن لا عقل له، قلت: جعلت فداك إن ممن يصف هذا الامر قوما لا بأس بهم عندنا وليست لهم تلك العقول فقال: ليس هؤلاء ممن خاطب الله إن الله خلق العقل فقال له: اقبل فاقبل وقال له: أدبر فأدبر، فقال: وعزتي وجلالي ما خلقت شيئا أحسن منك أو احب إلي منك، بك آخذ وبك اعطي
records.thaqalaynSanad: 6. [6/6] Usul al-Kafi: Abu Abdillah al-Asimi from Ali b. al-Hasan from Ali b. Asbat from al-Hasan b. al-Jahm from Abi al-Hasan al-Ridha عليه السلام. He (al-Asimi) said: Our companions (the Shias) and intellect were mentioned in his presence, so he عليه السلام said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: consideration is not given to a man of religion who has no intellect. I said: may I be made your ransom, from those who claim this affair (Wilaya) there are some who do mean well but do not have that level of intellect, so he said: such people are not the ones addressed by Allāh, verily Allāh created the intellect and said to it: draw near, so it drew near, and said to it: turn back, so it turned back, then He said: by my power and majesty I have not created a thing better than you or more beloved to me than you, by you do I take and by you do I give.

records.chapter: Sign of Perfection of Intellect
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad Āṣif al-Muḥsinī
records.englishText: 1. [1/7] Usul al-Kafi: Muhammad b. Yahya from Ahmad b. Muhammad from Ibn Mahbub from Abdallah b. Sinan who said: I mentioned a person who was afflicted by forgetfulness in Wudhu and Salat to Abu Abdillah عليه السلام and added: He is a person with intellect. Abu Abdillah عليه السلام said: and what kind of intellect does he have while he is obeying Satan? I said to him: how is he obeying Satan? he said: ask him – this that comes over him what is its cause? he will tell you it is from the handiwork of Satan.
records.arabicText: [1/7] اصول الكافي: محمد بن يحيى، عن أحمد بن محمد، عن ابن محبوب، عن عبدالله بن سنان قال: ذكرت لابي عبدالله عليه السلام رجلا مبتلى بالوضوء والصلاة وقلت: هو رجل عاقل، فقال: أبوعبدالله وأي عقل له وهو يطيع الشيطان؟ فقلت له: وكيف يطيع الشيطان؟ فقال سله هذا الذي ياتيه من أي شئ هو؟ فإنه يقول لك من عمل الشيطان
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. [1/7] Usul al-Kafi: Muhammad b. Yahya from Ahmad b. Muhammad from Ibn Mahbub from Abdallah b. Sinan who said: I mentioned a person who was afflicted by forgetfulness in Wudhu and Salat to Abu Abdillah عليه السلام and added: He is a person with intellect. Abu Abdillah عليه السلام said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: and what kind of intellect does he have while he is obeying Satan? I said to him: how is he obeying Satan? he said: ask him – this that comes over him what is its cause? he will tell you it is from the handiwork of Satan.

records.chapter: Sign of Perfection of Intellect
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad Āṣif al-Muḥsinī
records.englishText: 2. [2/8] al-Khisal: From his father (i.e. Ali b. al-Husayn b. Babawayh) from Sa’d b. Abdallah from Ahmad b. Abi Abdillah al-Barqi from al-Hasan b. Ali b. Fadhal from Tha’laba b. Maymun from Abi Abdillah عليه السلام who said: the commander of the faithful عليه السلام said: men are of three kinds: intelligent, foolish and corrupt sinner, as for the intelligent one the religion is his path, and forbearance is his habit, and giving counsel is his disposition, when he is asked he answers, and when he speaks he is to the point, and when he hears he comprehends, and when he reports he is truthful, and when one confides in him he is loyal, as for the fool if someone alerts him to a beautiful thing he overlooks it, and if he is dissuaded from good he acquiesces to forsake it, and if he is driven to ignorance he becomes ignorant, and if he reports he is incorrect, he does not understand and if perchance he is able to understand he does not learn from it, and as for the corrupt sinner if you rely on him he betrays you, and if you befriend him he hates you, and if you trust him he is not sincere with you.
records.arabicText: [2/8] الخصال: عن أبيه عن سعد بن عبد الله، عن أحمد أبي عبد الله البرقي، عن الحسن بن علي بن فضال، عن ثعلبة بن ميمون، عن أبي عبد الله عليه السلام قال : قال أمير المؤمنين عليه السلام : الرجال ثلاثة : عاقل وأحمق وفاجر، فالعاقل الدين شريعته، والحلم طبيعته، والرأي سجيته، إن سئل أجاب ، وإن تكلم أصاب، وإن سمع وعى، وإن حدث صدق، وإن اطمأن إليه أحد وفى، والاحمق إن استنبه بجميل غفل، وإن استنزل عن حسن نزل، وإن حمل على جهل جهل، وإن حدث كذب، لا يفقه وإن فقه لا يتفقه، والفاجر إن ائتمنته خانك، وإن صاحبته شانك وإن وثقت به لم ينصحك
records.thaqalaynSanad: 2. [2/8] al-Khisal: From his father (i.e. Ali b. al-Husayn b. Babawayh) from Sa’d b. Abdallah from Ahmad b. Abi Abdillah al-Barqi from al-Hasan b. Ali b. Fadhal from Tha’laba b. Maymun from Abi Abdillah عليه السلام who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: the commander of the faithful عليه السلام said: men are of three kinds: intelligent, foolish and corrupt sinner, as for the intelligent one the religion is his path, and forbearance is his habit, and giving counsel is his disposition, when he is asked he answers, and when he speaks he is to the point, and when he hears he comprehends, and when he reports he is truthful, and when one confides in him he is loyal, as for the fool if someone alerts him to a beautiful thing he overlooks it, and if he is dissuaded from good he acquiesces to forsake it, and if he is driven to ignorance he becomes ignorant, and if he reports he is incorrect, he does not understand and if perchance he is able to understand he does not learn from it, and as for the corrupt sinner if you rely on him he betrays you, and if you befriend him he hates you, and if you trust him he is not sincere with you.

records.chapter: Merit of Knowledge and the Scholar and the Obligation of Knowledge
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad Āṣif al-Muḥsinī
records.englishText: 1. [1/9] al-Khisal: From his father (Ali b. al-Husayn b. Babawayh) from Ali from his father (Ibrahim b. Hashim) from Ibn Maymun from Ja’far b. Muhammad from his forefathers from Ali عليه السلام who said: the messenger of Allāh صلى الله عليه وآله said: the merit of knowledge is more beloved to Allāh than the merit of worship. The best of your religion is self-restraint (from sins).
records.arabicText: [1/9] الخصال: عن أبيه، عن علي، عن أبيه، عن ابن ميمون، عن جعفر بن محمد، عن آبائه، عن علي عليه السلام قال : قال رسول الله صلى الله عليه وآله: فضل العلم أحب إلى الله من فضل العبادة وأفضل دينكم الورع
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. [1/9] al-Khisal: From his father (Ali b. al-Husayn b. Babawayh) from Ali from his father (Ibrahim b. Hashim) from Ibn Maymun from Ja’far b. Muhammad from his forefathers from Ali عليه السلام who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: the messenger of Allāh صلى الله عليه وآله said: the merit of knowledge is more beloved to Allāh than the merit of worship. The best of your religion is self-restraint (from sins).

records.chapter: Merit of Knowledge and the Scholar and the Obligation of Knowledge
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad Āṣif al-Muḥsinī
records.englishText: 2. [2/10] Usul al-Kafi: From Ali from his father from Ibn Abi Umayr AND Muhammad b. Yahya from Ahmad b. Muhammad from Ibn Abi Umayr from Sayf b. Umayra from Abi Hamza from Abi Ja’far عليه السلام who said: the scholar whose knowledge benefits others is better than seventy thousand worshippers.
records.arabicText: [2/10] اصول الكافي: عن علي، عن أبيه، عن ابن أبي عمير، وعن محمد بن يحيى، عن أحمد ابن محمد، عن ابن أبي عمير، عن سيف بن عميرة، عن أبي حمزة، عن أبي جعفر عليه السلام قال: عالم ينتفع بعلمه أفضل من سبعين ألف عابد
records.thaqalaynSanad: 2. [2/10] Usul al-Kafi: From Ali from his father from Ibn Abi Umayr AND Muhammad b. Yahya from Ahmad b. Muhammad from Ibn Abi Umayr from Sayf b. Umayra from Abi Hamza from Abi Ja’far عليه السلام who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: the scholar whose knowledge benefits others is better than seventy thousand worshippers.

records.chapter: Merit of Knowledge and the Scholar and the Obligation of Knowledge
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad Āṣif al-Muḥsinī
records.englishText: 3. [3/11] al-Kafi: From Ali from Muhammad b. Isa from Yunus from Jamil from Abi Abdillah عليه السلام – he (Jamil) said: I heard him saying: The people rise up (every morning) being in one of three categories: knowledgeable scholar, student seeking knowledge or scum. So we are the scholars and our Shia are the students and the rest of the people are scum.
records.arabicText: [3/11] الكافي: عن علي، عن محمد بن عيسى، عن يونس، عن جميل، عن أبي عبد الله عليه السلام قال: سمعته يقول: يغدو الناس على ثلاثة أصناف عالم ومتعلم وغثاء فنحن العلماء وشيعتنا المتعلمون وسائر الناس غثاء
records.thaqalaynSanad: 3. [3/11] al-Kafi: From Ali from Muhammad b. Isa from Yunus from Jamil from Abi Abdillah عليه السلام – he (Jamil) said: I heard him saying:
records.thaqalaynMatn: The people rise up (every morning) being in one of three categories: knowledgeable scholar, student seeking knowledge or scum. So we are the scholars and our Shia are the students and the rest of the people are scum.

records.chapter: Merit of Knowledge and the Scholar and the Obligation of Knowledge
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad Āṣif al-Muḥsinī
records.englishText: 4. [4/12] al-Khisal: From Ibn al-Walid from al-Saffar from Ibn Isa from al-Washsha from Ahmad b. Aidh from Abi Khadija from Abi Abdillah عليه السلام who said: The people rise up (every morning) being in one of three categories: (then he mentioned its like) …
records.arabicText: [4/12] الخصال: عن ابن الوليد، عن الصفار، عن ابن عيسى، عن الوشاء، عن احمد بن عائذ، عن ابي خديجة، عن ابي عبدالله عليه السلام قال: الناس يغدون على ثلاثة (ثم ذكر مثله)
records.thaqalaynSanad: 4. [4/12] al-Khisal: From Ibn al-Walid from al-Saffar from Ibn Isa from al-Washsha from Ahmad b. Aidh from Abi Khadija from Abi Abdillah عليه السلام who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: The people rise up (every morning) being in one of three categories: (then he mentioned its like) …

records.chapter: Merit of Knowledge and the Scholar and the Obligation of Knowledge
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad Āṣif al-Muḥsinī
records.englishText: 5. [5/13] al-Kafi: From Muhammad b. Yahya from Ahmad from Ibn Mahbub from Abi Ayyub al-Khazzaz from Sulayman b. Khalid from Abi Abdillah عليه السلام who said: no one dies from among the believers whose death is more pleasing to Iblis than the death of a Faqih.
records.arabicText: [5/13] الكافي: عن محمد بن يحيى، عن أحمد، عن ابن محبوب، عن أبي أيوب الخزاز، عن سليمان بن خالد، عن أبي عبدالله عليه السلام قال: ما من أحد يموت من المؤمنين أحب إلى إبليس من موت فقيه
records.thaqalaynSanad: 5. [5/13] al-Kafi: From Muhammad b. Yahya from Ahmad from Ibn Mahbub from Abi Ayyub al-Khazzaz from Sulayman b. Khalid from Abi Abdillah عليه السلام who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: no one dies from among the believers whose death is more pleasing to Iblis than the death of a Faqih.

records.chapter: Merit of Knowledge and the Scholar and the Obligation of Knowledge
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad Āṣif al-Muḥsinī
records.englishText: 6. [6/14] al-Khisal: From Ibn al-Walid from al-Saffar from Ibn Isa from Ibn Mahbub from Abdallah b. Sinan who said: I heard Aba Abdillah عليه السلام saying: I do have mercy on three and they do indeed deserve mercy: a man of standing who has been stricken with lowliness after his former high position, a man of riches who has been stricken with need after his former self-sufficiency, and a scholar who is belittled by his family and the ignorant.
records.arabicText: [6/14] الخصال: عن ابن الوليد، عن الصفار، عن ابن عيسى، عن ابن محبوب، عن عبد الله بن سنان قال: سمعت أبا عبد الله عليه السلام يقول: إني لارحم ثلاثة وحق لهم أن يرحموا: عزير أصابته مذلة بعد العز، وغني أصابته حاجة بعد الغنى، وعالم يستخف به أهله والجهلة
records.thaqalaynSanad: 6. [6/14] al-Khisal: From Ibn al-Walid from al-Saffar from Ibn Isa from Ibn Mahbub from Abdallah b. Sinan who said: I heard Aba Abdillah عليه السلام saying:
records.thaqalaynMatn: I do have mercy on three and they do indeed deserve mercy: a man of standing who has been stricken with lowliness after his former high position, a man of riches who has been stricken with need after his former self-sufficiency, and a scholar who is belittled by his family and the ignorant.

records.chapter: Merit of Knowledge and the Scholar and the Obligation of Knowledge
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad Āṣif al-Muḥsinī
records.englishText: 7. [7/15] al-Kafi: The messenger of Allāh صلى الله عليه وآله said: seeking knowledge is compulsory on every Muslim.
records.arabicText: [7/15] الكافي: قال رسول الله صلى الله عليه وآله: طلب العلم فريضة على كل مسلم
records.thaqalaynSanad: 7. [7/15] al-Kafi: The messenger of Allāh صلى الله عليه وآله said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: seeking knowledge is compulsory on every Muslim.

records.chapter: Merit of Knowledge and the Scholar and the Obligation of Knowledge
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad Āṣif al-Muḥsinī
records.englishText: 8. [8/16] al-Khisal: My father from Sa’d from al-Barqi from his father from Safwan from al-Khazzaz from Muhammad b. Muslim and others apart from him from Abi Abdillah عليه السلام who said: the messenger of Allāh صلى الله عليه وآله said: start the day either as a scholar or as a student or loving the scholars and do not be a fourth for you will perish because of your hatred towards them (i.e. the scholars).
records.arabicText: [8/16] الخصال: أبي، عن سعد، عن البرقي، عن أبيه، عن صفوان، عن الخزاز، عن محمد بن مسلم وغيره، عن أبي عبد الله عليه السلام قال: قال رسول الله صلى الله عليه وآله: اغد عالما أو متعلما أو أحب العلماء ولا تكن رابعا فتهلك ببغضهم
records.thaqalaynSanad: 8. [8/16] al-Khisal: My father from Sa’d from al-Barqi from his father from Safwan from al-Khazzaz from Muhammad b. Muslim and others apart from him from Abi Abdillah عليه السلام who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: the messenger of Allāh صلى الله عليه وآله said: start the day either as a scholar or as a student or loving the scholars and do not be a fourth for you will perish because of your hatred towards them (i.e. the scholars).

records.chapter: Merit of Knowledge and the Scholar and the Obligation of Knowledge
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad Āṣif al-Muḥsinī
records.englishText: 9. [9/17] al-Kafi: Muhammad b. Ismail from al-Fadhl b. Shadhan from Ibn Abi Umayr from Jamil b. Salih from Aban b. Taghlib from Abi Abdillah عليه السلام who said: I would have even liked it that my companions’ heads are lashed with whips for them to gain a comprehensive understanding (of the religion).
records.arabicText: [9/17] الكافي: محمد بن إسماعيل، عن الفضل بن شاذان، عن ابن أبي عمير، عن جميل بن دراج، عن أبان بن تغلب، عن أبي عبدالله عليه السلام قال: لوددت أن أصحابي ضربت رؤوسهم بالسياط حتى يتفقهوا
records.thaqalaynSanad: 9. [9/17] al-Kafi: Muhammad b. Ismail from al-Fadhl b. Shadhan from Ibn Abi Umayr from Jamil b. Salih from Aban b. Taghlib from Abi Abdillah عليه السلام who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: I would have even liked it that my companions’ heads are lashed with whips for them to gain a comprehensive understanding (of the religion).

records.chapter: Merit of Knowledge and the Scholar and the Obligation of Knowledge
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad Āṣif al-Muḥsinī
records.englishText: 10. [10/-] al-Khisal: In the ‘Four Hundred’ Narration: the commander of the faithful عليه السلام said: teach your children that which will benefit them – the Murjia should not overpower them with their speculative opinions.
records.arabicText: [-/10] الخصال: في حديث الأربعمائة: قال أمير المؤمنين عليه السلام: علموا صبيانكم ما ينفعهم به، لا يغلب عليهم المرجئة برأيها
records.thaqalaynSanad: 10. [10/-] al-Khisal: In the ‘Four Hundred’ Narration: the commander of the faithful عليه السلام said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: teach your children that which will benefit them – the Murjia should not overpower them with their speculative opinions.

records.chapter: Reward of the Scholar and the Seeker of Knowledge
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad Āṣif al-Muḥsinī
records.englishText: 1. [1/18] al-Kafi: Muhammad b. al-Hasan and Ali b. Muhammad from Sahl b. Ziyad; Muhammad b. Yahya from Ahmad b. Muhammad – all together from Ja’far b. Muhammad al-Ash’ari from Abdallah b. Maymun al-Qaddah; and Ali b. Ibrahim from his father from Hammad b. Isa from al-Qaddah from Abi Abdillah (AS) who said: "The Messenger of Allāh (sw) said: whoever treads a path seeking knowledge, Allāh makes him tread a path to heaven. The angels lay down their wings for the seeker of knowledge, being pleased with him. They do seek forgiveness for the seeker of knowledge whatsoever is in heaven and whatsoever is on earth, even the fish in the sea. The excellence of the scholar over the worshipper is like the excellence of the moon over the rest of the stars on a full-moon night. The scholars are the inheritors of the prophets, the prophets do not leave behind silver or gold coins, rather they leave behind knowledge. So, whomsoever partakes of it then he has obtained a fortunate share."
records.arabicText: [1/18] الكافي: محمد بن الحسن وعلي بن محمد، عن سهل بن زياد، ومحمد بن يحيى، عن أحمد بن محمد جميعا، عن جعفر بن محمد الاشعري، عن عبدالله بن ميمون القداح، وعلي بن إبراهيم، عن أبيه، عن حماد بن عيسى، عن القداح، عن أبي عبدالله عليه السلام قال: قال رسول الله صلى الله عليه وآله: من سلك طريقا يطلب فيه علما سلك الله به طريقا إلى الجنة وإن الملائكة لتضع أجنحتها لطالب العلم رضا به وإنه يستغفر لطالب العلم من في السماء ومن في الارض حتى الحوت في البحر، وفضل العالم على العابد كفضل القمر على سائر النجوم ليلة البدر، وإن العلماء ورثة الانبياء إن الانبياء لم يورثوا دينارا ولا درهما ولكن ورثوا العلم فمن أخذ منه أخذ بحظ وافر
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. [1/18] al-Kafi: Muhammad b. al-Hasan and Ali b. Muhammad from Sahl b. Ziyad; Muhammad b. Yahya from Ahmad b. Muhammad – all together from Ja’far b. Muhammad al-Ash’ari from Abdallah b. Maymun al-Qaddah; and Ali b. Ibrahim from his father from Hammad b. Isa from al-Qaddah from Abi Abdillah (AS) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The Messenger of Allāh (sw) said: whoever treads a path seeking knowledge, Allāh makes him tread a path to heaven. The angels lay down their wings for the seeker of knowledge, being pleased with him. They do seek forgiveness for the seeker of knowledge whatsoever is in heaven and whatsoever is on earth, even the fish in the sea. The excellence of the scholar over the worshipper is like the excellence of the moon over the rest of the stars on a full-moon night. The scholars are the inheritors of the prophets, the prophets do not leave behind silver or gold coins, rather they leave behind knowledge. So, whomsoever partakes of it then he has obtained a fortunate share."

records.chapter: Reward of the Scholar and the Seeker of Knowledge
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad Āṣif al-Muḥsinī
records.englishText: 2. [2/19] al-Kafi: Muhammad b. Yahya from Ahmad b. Muhammad from al-Hasan b. Mahbub from Jamil b. Salih from Muhammad b. Muslim from Abi Ja’far عليه السلام who said: the one who teaches the knowledge from among you has an equivalent reward with the student who seeks knowledge except that he (the teacher) has a degree over him (the student), so seek knowledge from the bearers of knowledge and teach it to your brothers in the same way as the scholars had taught it to you.
records.arabicText: [2/19] الكافي: محمد بن يحيى، عن أحمد بن محمد، عن الحسن بن محبوب، عن جميل بن صالح عن محمد بن مسلم، عن أبي جعفر عليه السلام قال: إن الذي يعلم العلم منكم له أجر مثل أجر المتعلم وله الفضل عليه، فتعلموا العلم من حملة العلم وعلموه إخوانكم كما علمكموه العلماء
records.thaqalaynSanad: 2. [2/19] al-Kafi: Muhammad b. Yahya from Ahmad b. Muhammad from al-Hasan b. Mahbub from Jamil b. Salih from Muhammad b. Muslim from Abi Ja’far عليه السلام who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: the one who teaches the knowledge from among you has an equivalent reward with the student who seeks knowledge except that he (the teacher) has a degree over him (the student), so seek knowledge from the bearers of knowledge and teach it to your brothers in the same way as the scholars had taught it to you.

records.chapter: Reward of the Scholar and the Seeker of Knowledge
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad Āṣif al-Muḥsinī
records.englishText: 3. [3/20] From the messenger of Allāh صلى الله عليه واله who said: whoever preserves forty of our narrations Allāh will raise him on the day of judgment as a scholar possessing understanding.
records.arabicText: [3/20] عن رسول الله صلى الله عليه واله: من حفظ احاديثنا أربعين حديثا بعثه الله يوم القيامة عالما فقيها
records.thaqalaynSanad: 3. [3/20] From the messenger of Allāh صلى الله عليه واله who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: whoever preserves forty of our narrations Allāh will raise him on the day of judgment as a scholar possessing understanding.

records.chapter: Reward of the Scholar and the Seeker of Knowledge
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad Āṣif al-Muḥsinī
records.englishText: 4. [4/21] al-Uyun: Via the three chains from al-Ridha from his forefathers عليهم السلام: the messenger of Allāh صلى الله عليه واله said: O Allāh have mercy on my successors – thrice – it was said to him: and who are your successors? He said: those who come after me and transmit my narrations and my Sunnahteaching it to the people after me.
records.arabicText: [4/21] العيون: بالاسانيد الثلاثة عن الرضاء عن ابائه عليهم السلام: قال رسول الله صلى الله عليه واله: اللهم ارحم خلفائي- ثلاث مرات – قيل له ومن خلفائك قال الذين يأتون من بعدي ويروون أحاديثي وسنتي فيعلمونها الناس من بعدي
records.thaqalaynSanad: 4. [4/21] al-Uyun: Via the three chains from al-Ridha from his forefathers عليهم السلام:
records.thaqalaynMatn: the messenger of Allāh صلى الله عليه واله said: O Allāh have mercy on my successors – thrice – it was said to him: and who are your successors? He said: those who come after me and transmit my narrations and my Sunnahteaching it to the people after me.

records.chapter: Reward of the Scholar and the Seeker of Knowledge
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad Āṣif al-Muḥsinī
records.englishText: 5. [5/22] Rijal al-Najashi: From our Shaykh al-Mufid in his book “Masabih al-Nur”: reported to us Ibn Qulawayh from Ali b. al-Husayn b. Babawayh from Abdallah b. Ja’far al-Himyari from Dawud b. al-Qasim al-Ja’fari who said: I presented the book “Yawm wa Layla” of Yunus to Abi Muhammad al-Askari عليه السلام, so he said to me: who authored this? I said: it has been authored by Yunus the client of the family of Yaqtin, he said: may Allāh give him for every letter a light on the day of judgment.
records.arabicText: [5/22] رجال النجاشي: عن شيخنا المفيد في كتابه مصابيح النور: أخبرني ابن قولويه، عن علي بن الحسين بن بابويه، عن عبد الله بن جعفر الحميري، عن داود بن القاسم الجعفري، قال: عرضت على أبي محمد صاحب العسكر عليه السلام كتاب يوم وليلة ليونس فقال لي تصنيف من هذا؟ فقلت: تصنيف يونس مولى آل يقطين فقال: أعطاه الله بكل حرف نورا يوم القيامة
records.thaqalaynSanad: 5. [5/22] Rijal al-Najashi: From our Shaykh al-Mufid in his book “Masabih al-Nur”: reported to us Ibn Qulawayh from Ali b. al-Husayn b. Babawayh from Abdallah b. Ja’far al-Himyari from Dawud b. al-Qasim al-Ja’fari who said: I presented the book “Yawm wa Layla” of Yunus to Abi Muhammad al-Askari عليه السلام, so he said to me: who authored this? I said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: it has been authored by Yunus the client of the family of Yaqtin, he said: may Allāh give him for every letter a light on the day of judgment.

records.chapter: Attributes of the Scholar
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad Āṣif al-Muḥsinī
records.englishText: 1. [1/23] al-Kafi: Muhammad b. Yahya al-Attar from Ahmad b. Muhammad b. Isa from al-Hasan b. Mahbub from Muawiya b. Wahb who said: I heard Aba Abdillah عليه السلام saying: seek knowledge and adorn yourself with forbearance and dignity together with it (knowledge), and humble yourself to the one you teach knowledge, and humble yourself to the one from whom you seek knowledge, and do not be tyrannical scholars so that your falsehood drives away your truth.
records.arabicText: [1/23] الكافي: محمد بن يحيى العطار، عن أحمد بن محمد بن عيسى، عن الحسن بن محبوب عن معاوية بن وهب قال: سمعت أبا عبدالله عليه السلام يقول: اطلبوا العلم وتزينوا معه بالحلم والوقار، وتواضعوا لمن تعلمونه العلم، وتواضعوا لمن طلبتم منه العلم، ولا تكونوا علماء جبارين فيذهب باطلكم بحقكم
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. [1/23] al-Kafi: Muhammad b. Yahya al-Attar from Ahmad b. Muhammad b. Isa from al-Hasan b. Mahbub from Muawiya b. Wahb who said: I heard Aba Abdillah عليه السلام saying:
records.thaqalaynMatn: seek knowledge and adorn yourself with forbearance and dignity together with it (knowledge), and humble yourself to the one you teach knowledge, and humble yourself to the one from whom you seek knowledge, and do not be tyrannical scholars so that your falsehood drives away your truth.

records.chapter: Attributes of the Scholar
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad Āṣif al-Muḥsinī
records.englishText: 2. [2/24] al-Kafi: Ali b. Ibrahim from Muhammad b. Isa from Yunus from Hammad b. Uthman from al-Harith b. al-Mughira al-Nasri from Abi Abdillah عليه السلام in regards the words of Allāh Mighty and Majestic: “Verily they truly fear Allāh from among his slaves – the scholars” (35:28) he said: He means by the scholars the one whose deeds conform with his words, as for the one whose deeds are contrary to his words then he is not a scholar.
records.arabicText: [2/24] الكافي: علي بن إبراهيم، عن محمد بن عيسى، عن يونس، عن حماد بن عثمان، عن الحارث بن المغيرة النصري، عن أبي عبدالله عليه السلام في قول الله عزوجل: ” إنما يخشى الله من عباده العلماء ” قال: يعني بالعلماء من صدق فعله قوله، ومن لم يصدق فعله قوله فليس بعالم
records.thaqalaynSanad: 2. [2/24] al-Kafi: Ali b. Ibrahim from Muhammad b. Isa from Yunus from Hammad b. Uthman from al-Harith b. al-Mughira al-Nasri from Abi Abdillah عليه السلام in regards the words of Allāh Mighty and Majestic: “Verily they truly fear Allāh from among his slaves – the scholars” (35:28)
records.thaqalaynMatn: he said: He means by the scholars the one whose deeds conform with his words, as for the one whose deeds are contrary to his words then he is not a scholar.
